title: malus asiatica
intro: Malus asiatica, also known as the Chinese crabapple, is a species of deciduous tree or shrub native to China and Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds. The health benefits of Malus asiatica include reducing fever, alleviating diarrhea, and relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Malus asiatica can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, as well as being used in combination with other herbs to create various traditional Chinese medicinal formulas.
